channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 2 hours.

item.title: 🌍 Ceasefire ‘stops’ War Powers clock on Iran, Hegseth claims | Defense News
item.description: The 60-day threshold is delineated in the War Powers Resolution of 1973. The most relevant part of that law makes no mention of a ceasefire.

item.title: 🏴 The View From Europe | Foreign Policy
item.description: Belgian Defense Minister Theo Francken on Hegseth, Hormuz, and NATO.

item.title: 🏛️ Navy, Marine Corps weighing force generation model revamp for amphibs | Breaking Defense
item.description: Navy and Marine Corps leaders said they are considering shaking up the 36-month Optimized Fleet Response plan, in lieu of a longer cycle that can accommodate two deployments.

item.title: 📍 Trump says ‘probably’ when asked if he might pull US troops out of Italy, Spain | Defense News
item.description: The comment came one day after Trump announced that Washington was looking at reducing the number of U.S. military personnel in Germany.

item.title: 🌍 Can Iraq’s new prime minister fix his country’s broken politics? | Atlantic Council
item.description: Ali al-Zaidi must contend with a growing crisis of legitimacy in the very system that brought him to power and the backing of a coalition that is increasingly unstable. The post Can Iraq’s new prime minister fix his country’s broken politics? appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 How the Trump administration can get even more out of its diplomacy with Lukashenka | Atlantic Council
item.description: The White House should combine outreach to the Belarusian dictator with greater engagement with Belarusian civil society. The post How the Trump administration can get even more out of its diplomacy with Lukashenka appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏛️ Pacific Marines will be first to test-drive new pilot-optional helicopter | Defense News
item.description: The service is “aggressively pursuing” an operational demo in 2027 for the Aerial Logistics Connector.

item.title: 🏛️ Reconciliation ‘floodgates’ about to open after slow start for contracts, Hegseth says | Breaking Defense
item.description: “Why do we suddenly have a two part budget where this committee and the Congress generally has oversight and input, to a process where a corner of the budget is essentially a slush fund?” asked Sen. Angus King, I-Maine, during a SASC hearing.

item.title: 🏴 Putin’s parade once projected power. Now it reveals Russia’s weakness. | Atlantic Council
item.description: Russia's annual Victory Day parade has traditionally been an opportunity for Putin to project power. This year, however, the parade has been scaled down amid fears of Ukrainian attack and risks becoming a symbol of Russian weakness, writes Peter Dickinson. The post Putin’s…

item.title: 🌍 Trump At A Crossroads For Continuing The War With Iran | The War Zone
item.description: The president is reportedly discussing new options for attacking Iran while facing a legal deadline on continuing the conflict. The post Trump At A Crossroads For Continuing The War With Iran appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 Australia can be region’s most-trusted cloud node – if governance catches up | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Microsoft’s A$25 billion investment in Australian AI and cloud infrastructure positions the country as a significant node in the Indo-Pacific’s trusted digital architecture. That creates a strategic opportunity – one Australia is well placed to ...

item.title: 🏛️ Marine commandant: Every combatant command has requested an amphibious ready group | Defense One
item.description: The demand is about twice as high as the Marine Corps’ goal of three deployed at any given time, Gen. Eric Smith said Thursday.

item.title: 🌍 Admin mum on whether Trump will seek to legalize Iran war | Defense One
item.description: The 60 days allowable by the War Powers Resolution is about to run out, but the defense secretary thinks a ceasefire has paused the clock.

item.title: 🌍 Iran War Nears 60-Day War Powers Deadline | Foreign Policy
item.description: But U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth argues that the cease-fire has paused the clock.

item.title: 🌏 Economic Fury and Claims of Victory | War on the Rocks
item.description: Welcome to The Adversarial. Every other week, we’ll provide you with expert analysis on America’s greatest challengers: China, Russia, Iran, North Korea, and jihadists. Read more below.***IranThree weeks since a ceasefire took effect, the United States and Iran have shifted from…

item.title: 🌍 Air Force’s top general: Supplemental funding needed to replace US aircraft lost in Iran | Defense One
item.description: The 2027 budget request was made before dozens of aircraft were destroyed during Epic Fury.

item.title: 🏛️ Air Force seeks to scrap its E-11 BACN fleet | Breaking Defense
item.description: An Air Force spokesperson told Breaking Defense following the hearing that there are seven E-11s in the current fleet, and confirmed that the plan is to replace them with the emerging DAF (Department of the Air Force) Battle Network.

item.title: 🏛️ Air Force Wants To Axe Its E-11A BACN Communications Jets | The War Zone
item.description: The Air Force wants space-based systems to take over from the E-11A in providing critical data fusion and communications relay capabilities. The post Air Force Wants To Axe Its E-11A BACN Communications Jets appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏴 Russia’s windfall from the Iran war is temporary. Ukraine’s isn’t. | Breaking Defense
item.description: Kyiv, unlike Moscow, has secured strategic advantages from the war in Iran, writes Artur Kalandarov.

item.title: ⚛️ Marine Amphibious Combat Vehicles To Get Missile-Swatting Active Protection Systems | The War Zone
item.description: Active Protection Systems will give Marine ACVs a critical new layer of defense against anti-armor threats that could also help down incoming drones. The post Marine Amphibious Combat Vehicles To Get Missile-Swatting Active Protection Systems appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Romania enters US counter-drone marketplace | Defense News
item.description: Romania will now be able to acquire counter-unmanned aerial system technology through the U.S.’s counter-drone marketplace, the Army said on Wednesday.

item.title: 📍 Giorgia Meloni’s Populist Formula Failed | Foreign Policy
item.description: The Italian prime minister hasn’t convincingly delivered the renewal she once promised.

item.title: 🌍 High-Rises Amid the Rubble in Syria | Foreign Policy
item.description: Who is Sharaa’s investment campaign really for?

item.title: 🏴 ‘Everything Depends on Our Attitudes’ | Foreign Policy
item.description: Belgian Defense Minister Theo Francken on Hegseth, Hormuz, and NATO’s future.

item.title: 🏛️ Marines to start development on Advanced Reconnaissance Vehicle Increment 2 in 2029 | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Increment 2 iteration will consist of a counter unmanned aerial system variant, a recovery variant and one for precision fires.

item.title: 🏴 Zelenskyy refuses Russian demands to surrender Ukraine’s vital fortress belt | Atlantic Council
item.description: Ukraine rejects Russian demands to withdraw from northern Donetsk province as part of a peace deal and insists the region's fortress belt is absolutely indispensable for the defense of the country as a whole, writes Marc Goedemans. The post Zelenskyy refuses Russian demands to…

item.title: 🏴 Insurgent Offensive in Mali Exposes the Deficiencies of Junta-led Security in the Sahel | Just Security
item.description: Recent militant attacks that killed Mali's defense minister challenge the proposition of the region's juntas that Russia could be a better security partner than the West. The post Insurgent Offensive in Mali Exposes the Deficiencies of Junta-led Security in the Sahel appeared…

item.title: 🏛️ From prototypes to production: US Air Force seeks nearly $1B for initial CCA procurement | Defense News
item.description: In a first, the Air Force is asking Congress to fund the purchase of Collaborative Combat Aircraft, marking the beginning of the “loyal wingman" era.

item.title: 🌍 Mythos, not the Iran war, is the most significant geopolitical warning of our time | Atlantic Council
item.description: Artificial intelligence is already accelerating life-changing breakthroughs, but it also poses generational risks. The post Mythos, not the Iran war, is the most significant geopolitical warning of our time appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: ⚛️ B-1B Seen Carrying ARRW Hypersonic Missile For The First Time | The War Zone
item.description: The B-1B will now serve to 2037, and ARRW has emerged out of purgatory in the latest Pentagon budget request. The post B-1B Seen Carrying ARRW Hypersonic Missile For The First Time appeared first on The War Zone.
